% check a.php
a.php:10:1: $name is of type: string (DEBUG)
a.php:12:1: $self is of type: Foo (DEBUG)

% check b.php
b.php:14:1: $name is of type: string (DEBUG)

# Error for a non-existing return type in a trait method
# must be reported against the trait file, not the using class.
% check bad-trait.php
bad-trait.php:7:18: method badMethod returns non-existing type Traits\NoSuchType

% check uses-bad-trait.php

-- a.php --
<?php

class Foo
{
	use Traits\Name;
}

$foo = new Foo;
$name = $foo->name;
#debugType $name
$self = $foo->identity();
#debugType $self
-- b.php --
<?php

class FooParent
{
	use Traits\Name;
}

class FooChild extends FooParent
{
}

$foo = new FooChild;
$name = $foo->name;
#debugType $name
-- t.php --
<?php

namespace Traits;

trait Name
{
	/** @var string */
	public $name;

	public function identity(): self;
}
-- bad-trait.php --
<?php

namespace Traits;

trait BadTrait
{
	public function badMethod(): NoSuchType;
}
-- uses-bad-trait.php --
<?php

class UsesIt
{
	use Traits\BadTrait;
}
